working_patterns: Structural pivot from uniform grid to SLSQP over (x_i, y_i, r_i) with analytic Jacobians for boundary constraints — jumped score from ~0.78 to ~0.99
Hexagonal-ish row-based initial layouts (e.g. 5-4-5-4-5-3 = 26, or 5-6-5-6-4) as starting points for SLSQP
Adding a post-solve _shrink_to_valid pass (boundary cap + uniform pairwise shrink) to guarantee feasibility against SLSQP numerical slack
Multiple initial layouts tried, best VALID one selected — robustness to layout-specific local minima
Per-constraint analytic Jacobians for boundary inequalities reduce inner-iteration cost
failing_patterns: Neither candidate implemented analytic Jacobians for the pairwise non-overlap constraints — likely limits how many restarts fit in the time budget
Score plateau at ~0.990983 across both top candidates suggests both converged to similar local optimum from similar hex seeds
Fixed row-count layouts (5-4-5-4-5-3) bias toward a specific symmetry class and miss alternative packings
hypotheses: The SoTA target (~2.6361) corresponds to a known unequal-radii or asymmetric packing, so uniform-radius hex seeds plus local SLSQP get close (~0.99) but cannot cross into the optimal basin without perturbation or global search
Individual Python-closure constraints (one per boundary/pair) dominate runtime via Python overhead, preventing more restarts within the time budget
The _shrink_to_valid uniform scale is slightly pessimistic — it shrinks ALL radii by the worst pair factor, leaving slack in non-binding circles that could be individually enlarged
Starting radii initialized uniformly (e.g. r_init=0.09) biases SLSQP toward near-equal-radius local optima; corner/edge circles in optimum are typically larger than interior ones
suggested_directions: Vectorize the pair non-overlap constraint as a single vector-valued inequality returning all C(26,2)=325 slacks at once, with a sparse analytic Jacobian — dramatically speeds up SLSQP
Add basin-hopping or random perturbation restarts (jitter centers by ~0.02, radii by ~0.01) around the best solution to escape the ~0.99 plateau
Try diverse initial layouts: 4-5-4-5-4-4, 3-5-4-5-4-5, staggered hex, known Packomania-style configs with larger corner circles
Replace uniform shrink with per-circle greedy radius maximization: after positions are fixed, each r_i = min(boundary_cap, min_j(dist_ij - r_j)) solved via LP or iterative propagation
Initialize radii non-uniformly (larger at corners/edges, smaller interior) to bias toward asymmetric optima
Add a final polishing pass that alternates: (a) fix radii, optimize centers to maximize min-slack; (b) fix centers, solve LP for max sum of radii
Use scipy.optimize with method='trust-constr' which handles larger constraint sets better than SLSQP and supports sparse Jacobians natively
